name: Mirror-Gate 125 GeV Boundary-Response Threshold
locked_interpretation: The approximately 125 GeV measurement is valid and is interpreted as Mirror-Gate boundary-response energy.
canonical_equation: E_MG = E4(q_G)-E4(q_0) = integral_Gamma grad(E4) dot dq
pressure_reduction: E_MG = integral_0^xi_G P_ext(xi) dxi; P_ext = P_K+P_E+P_M+P_T+P_cross, with signed contributions
gate_condition: q_G is the first minimum-work state whose forward update crosses from the original orientation basin into the mirrored basin; saddle crossing and lambda_min=0 loss-of-hold are distinct candidates.
mass_relation: same four-interaction architecture, different observable; m_eff != E_MG/c^2 as mechanism
blocker: independent microscopic lattice energy calibration and derived deformation path
calibration_routes: 125 GeV anchor -> predict other masses, thresholds, and release relations
failure_conditions: omit one of the four interactions or cross-couplings
choose gate endpoint from the 125 GeV answer
use 125 GeV as calibration and also call it a prediction
energy_accounting: W_drive = E_MG + E_diss; current 125 GeV interpretation maps to stored/released E_MG, while E_diss must predict width/timing/loss.
claim_gate_detail: GREEN (One-Wave interpretation and pressure-work form) / YELLOW (first-principles numerical derivation and collider distributions)
